stories.headline: At least 17 people killed in Russia’s deadliest attack on Ukraine this year
stories.summary: More than 100 injured across country after Russia launches nearly 700 drones and dozens of ballistic and cruise missilesEurope live – latest updatesRussia has carried out its deadliest attack against Ukraine this year, killing at least 17 people and injuring more than 100 in a wave of drone and missile strikes across the country.Nine people died in the southern port city of Odesa and four were killed in Kyiv, including a 12-year-old boy. stories.headline: Russia pummels Ukraine with drone and missile strikes, killing at least 12
stories.summary: Missile and drone attacks on the port city of Odesa killed six people, with other fatalities recorded in Kyiv and DniproRussian strikes killed at least 12 people in Ukraine, local authorities have said, after Moscow pummelled its neighbour in overnight attacks.Missile and drone attacks on the southern port city of Odesa killed six people, the head of the city’s military administration, Sergiy Lysak, wrote on Telegram on Thursday. stories.headline: US blockade of Iranian ports to last 'as long as it takes,' Defence Secretary Hegseth says
stories.summary: The US naval blockade of Iran’s ports came into effect on Monday, with President Donald Trump warning that any attack ships would be “eliminated” if they attempted to break it.

stories.headline: Russia ‘does not deserve’ lifting of sanctions, Zelenskyy says, after deadly overnight strikes in Ukraine – as it happened

stories.headline: Russia launches deadliest aerial attack in months, killing 18 in Ukraine

stories.summary: President Donald Trump said Israel and Lebanon had agreed to a 10-day ceasefire, a move that offers to ease tensions more broadly in the region while the US and Iran are in discussions to extend their own truce ahead of its expiration next week.

stories.headline: Hegseth says US to keep Iran port blockade in Hormuz, ready for new attacks
stories.summary: US urges resolution through talks, but says Washington 'locked and loaded' to resume attacks.

stories.headline: Is Iran’s economy buckling under war pressure or holding up?
stories.summary: The Iran war has deepened the damage to its sanctions-hit economy, but oil revenues have provided a crucial cushion.

stories.headline: Europe has six weeks of jet fuel left caused by 'dire strait' crisis, IEA chief warns
stories.summary: Last week, the Airports Council International Europe wrote to the European Commission, saying jet fuel shortages could begin at the start of May if tankers do not start passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.summary: The International Monetary Fund said Egypt’s economic reforms and stronger buffers are enabling the country to better weather external shocks, as the local pound reverses some losses after the Iran war ceasefire.

stories.headline: Stock Rally Pauses as Traders Weigh Mixed Earnings, Iran War
stories.summary: Stocks fluctuated Thursday morning as oil prices gained and traders weighed whether a fragile ceasefire in the Iran war may be extended and mixed first-quarter earnings results.

stories.headline: CD&R-Backed Multi-Color Wins Approval of Debt Restructuring
stories.summary: Private equity backed Multi-Color Corp won court approval for a restructuring plan that cuts nearly $4 billion in debt, clearing the way for the label maker to exit Chapter 11 after striking a deal with a dissenting lender group.

stories.headline: Private Credit Funds Face More Pressure as Banks Swap Collateral
stories.summary: For years, Wall Street banks eagerly helped private credit funds amplify their investing firepower with hundreds of billions of dollars in loans, helping them notch ever-higher returns.

stories.headline: Amalgamated CEO: Rates Aren’t Everything
stories.summary: Amalgamated Bank CEO Priscilla Sims Brown joined Bloomberg Open Interest to break down why her bank is thriving in a “higher for longer” rate world. She reveals why loyal, values-driven clients matter more than interest rates, highlights booming demand in clean energy lending, and hints at a wave of potential bank mergers ahead. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: BofA Looks to Raise at Least $8.5 Billion From Bond Offering
stories.summary: Bank of America Corp. is seeking to raise at least $8.5 billion from a high-grade bond sale, according to people with direct knowledge of the matter, extending a borrowing spree by major Wall Street lenders this week.

stories.headline: SEC Asks for Input on Slimming CAT Trade Data Collection, Costs
stories.summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission asked for industry input on how to trim down the cost and scope of trading data that exchanges and brokers report to a centralized database.

stories.headline: Nine killed in second Turkish school shooting in two days

stories.headline: Magyar calls for fast handover of power in talks with Hungary's president
stories.summary: Péter Magyar, the man who ended Viktor Orbán's 16 years of continuous rule, wants the new parliament to convene in early May.

stories.headline: Trump says China has agreed not to send weapons to Iran
stories.summary: The president made the claim, which Beijing has yet to confirm, amid anger in China that its vessels could be caught up in a U.S. blockade targeting Iranian ports.

stories.headline: Spain approves plan to give around 500,000 undocumented migrants legal status
stories.summary: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez said migrants helped "build the rich, open and diverse Spain that we are and to which we aspire".

stories.headline: Founder of China's Evergrande pleads guilty to fraud
stories.summary: Evergrande was once China's biggest real estate firm, with a stock market valuation of more than $50bn.

stories.headline: Israelis war-weary but most oppose Iran ceasefire, poll suggests

stories.headline: The US blockade of Iran is a gamble. Will it work?

stories.headline: Italian PM condemns ally Trump over 'unacceptable' Pope criticism
